title: Vietnam
subtitle: 75 days · Saigon to Hanoi
body:
Vietnam has been, arguably, one of the easiest places to cycle. The coastal riding stays mostly flat, with wide roads, usually dotted with small towns and an endless supply of roadside food at various standards of hygiene. Bánh mìs or a bowl of phở being the go-to option for refueling.

Although coastal riding will begin to feel monotonous, eventually you find yourself craving a change in pace, in scenery, and in elevation. So you venture into the highlands… and into the karst… (WE LOVE KARST)

As for road conditions, riding is fairly comfortable most of the time. There are many instances where you'll find yourself on an overdeveloped thoroughfare all to yourself and a few scooters (or cows). In contrast, there will be other instances where you have no choice but to ride on the major highways, alongside the trucks and their colorfully obnoxious horns.

Accommodations:

I'll admit, I didn't camp once. With the cost of living — especially by Western standards — it just didn't make sense. Stealth camping can be possible, but I spoiled myself with motel showers and hard mattresses.

Human Experience:

The people are incredibly kind and endlessly welcoming. And the children all seem to know the same three phrases, excited to try them out: "What's your name?", "Where are you from?", "How old are you?"

I also happened to be riding during Tết, Vietnam's Lunar New Year. For about 10 days, the country slows to a near standstill… shops close, families gather, and the usual rhythm disappears. I found myself stuck in Da Nang, not wanting to risk ending up in the middle of nowhere without food or water.

Resources:

Bike shops are limited, so you need to ensure you properly stock up on tubes when possible… or bottom brackets 🥴

If it's not a local shop, WinMart became the staple option for resupply. Choices in fuel are limited.

My pace slowed down considerably once I reached Southeast Asia… At close to two and a half months, I did not expect to stay in Vietnam this long.

7/10
